name: United Security Bank
description_short: United Security Bank merged with Community West Bank in April 2016 to form a stronger community bank serving Central California with consumer and business banking services.
description_long:
United Security Bank operated as a community bank before merging with Community West Bank, effective April 1, 2016. The merger was designed to create a stronger regional financial institution focused on serving Central California communities. Following the consolidation, the entity now operates under the Community West Bank name while maintaining the infrastructure and services previously offered by United Security Bank. The bank positioned itself as a community-focused institution committed to building stronger local communities rather than operating as a large national or global institution.

The bank offers standard retail and commercial banking services including consumer online banking, e-business solutions for commercial customers, mobile banking, and online account management. Customers can access deposit accounts, check services, and account management tools through multiple channels including web platforms and mobile applications. The bank provides FDIC insurance on eligible deposits, protecting customer funds up to applicable limits. Additional services include mortgage products, lost and stolen card replacement, and online statements.

United Security Bank distinguished itself through its community-oriented approach and commitment to Central California. The bank invested in accessibility features, including screen-reader compatibility for visually impaired users, demonstrating commitment to inclusive banking services. The organization maintained dedicated customer support through phone and live chat channels. The merger with Community West Bank was presented as a strategic move to enhance service capabilities and financial strength rather than a distress situation.

The primary limitation is that detailed information about specific products, rates, fees, and competitive positioning is not available on the provided website content. The site functions primarily as a portal directing users to log into existing accounts or access general banking information, rather than providing comprehensive product details. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly or visit in person to obtain specific information about account types, interest rates, lending terms, and fee schedules. The website's focus on account access rather than marketing information limits assessment of how this institution compares to competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Lost and stolen card replacement services
Online banking agreement and security features
pros: FDIC insurance protection on eligible deposits
Multiple access channels including online banking, mobile banking, and live chat support
Community bank model focused on Central California regional service
Accessibility features including screen-reader support for visually impaired users
E-business solutions available for commercial customers
Merger with Community West Bank strengthened financial stability and service offerings
cons: Website provides minimal product details, rates, or fee information for comparison shopping
No specific information about loan products, interest rates, or account minimums disclosed
Limited geographic footprint restricted to Central California only
Merged entity now operates under Community West Bank name, creating potential customer confusion
Website emphasizes account login rather than new customer acquisition information
best_for: Central California residents seeking community bank relationships
Small to medium-sized businesses in the region requiring commercial banking services
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits and local customer service
Users needing accessible banking with screen-reader compatibility
diagnosis: United Security Bank is best for Central California residents and business customers seeking traditional community bank services with FDIC insurance and accessible customer support. The main caveat is that the bank has merged with Community West Bank (as of April 2016) and now operates under that name, and the website provides insufficient detail about specific products, rates, and fees to make informed product comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
